title: Ergonomic spray actuator and a method of dispensing a composition using a spray actuator
abstract: An actuator is provided. The actuator includes a nozzle; a valve; a conduit in fluid communication with the valve at one end portion and in fluid communication with the nozzle at an opposite end portion; and a trigger (28) operatively connected with the valve, wherein the trigger includes a first zone (28a) and a second zone (28b), wherein the first and second zones are independently movable about a common axis (C).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An actuator comprising: a nozzle; a valve; a conduit in fluid communication with the valve at one end portion and in fluid communication with the nozzle at an opposite end portion; and a trigger operatively connected with the valve, wherein the trigger comprises a first zone and a second zone, wherein the first and second zones are independently movable about a common axis. 2. The actuator of claim 1, wherein the first zone is positionable at a variable angle from the second zone relative to the common axis. 3. The actuator of claim 1 or 2, wherein the trigger comprises a user selectable angle between the first and second zones relative to the common axis. 4. The actuator of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second zones of the trigger are configurable in at least a first position and a second position, wherein in the first position the first zone is radially offset from the second zone by a first angle, wherein in the second position the first zone is radially offset from the second zone by a second angle that is different from the first angle. 5. The actuator of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second zones are movable in unison. 6. The actuator of any of the preceding claims, wherein the trigger comprises a secondary coupler, wherein the secondary coupler at least partially joins the first and second zones of the trigger. 7. The actuator of any of the preceding claims, wherein the secondary coupler comprises an elastomeric material. 8. The actuator of any of the preceding claims, wherein the secondary coupler sets a predetermined angle between the first and second zones. 9. The actuator of any of the preceding claims, wherein the trigger comprises an energy storing coupling that joins the first and second zones. 10. The actuator of any of the preceding claims, wherein the energy storing coupling is selected from the group consisting of: torsion spring, coil spring, leaf spring, elastomeric material, and combinations thereof. 11. A method of dispensing a composition from a spray product, the method comprising the steps of: providing a spray product comprising: a nozzle; a valve; a conduit in fluid communication with the valve at one end portion and in fluid communication with the nozzle at an opposite end portion; and a trigger operatively connected with the valve, wherein the trigger comprises a first zone and a second zone, wherein the first and second zones are independently movable about a common axis; simultaneously actuating the first and second zones of the trigger from a forward position to a rearward position; selecting an angle between the first and second zones at a target angle relative to the common axis; and moving the first and second zones of the trigger in unison to maintain the target angle. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the first zone is positionable at a variable angle from the second zone relative to the common axis. 13. The method of claim 11 or 12, wherein the trigger comprises a user selectable angle between the first and second zones relative to the common axis. 14. The method of any of claims 11-13, wherein the first and second zones of the trigger are configurable in at least a first position and a second position, wherein in the first position the first 

 zone is radially offset from the second zone by a first angle, wherein in the second position the first zone is radially offset from the second zone by a second angle that is different from the first angle. 15. The method of any of claims 11-14, wherein the first and second zones are movable in unison.
